# sixtom

> Pair Sprints by Sam D'Angelo, lead engineer at Made In Cookware ($200M/yr DTC). Vibe coding gets you to 90%. The last 10% — production-readiness, the shape that scales across roles — is a different muscle. That's what I bring. $7,500 fixed. One client per month.

## What it is

- One week. You bring a domain expert.
- We pair on the thing they've been close on but can't quite get over.
- They keep the code. You keep the muscle.

## Who it's for

- Teams with a working prototype that won't ship.
- Internal tools that won't scale past the person who built them.
- Domain experts who can prototype with AI but stall on production.

## What I've built

- Our centralized ops tool at Made In Cookware. Every department, two months.
- A LinkedIn pipeline (Telegram bot → AI synthesis → scheduler) that posts from my work in progress.
- A pattern: take a vibe-coded 30k-line draft, ship a 10k-line version that's tested, shaped, and reusable.

## Pricing

- $7,500 USD, fixed.
- One client per month, by appointment.
- If we're not on track by day 3, we stop. You keep what we built and pay only for the time.

## How to engage

- Book a 30-min intro call (link on the homepage).
- Notify list for the next open slot (form on the homepage).
